title: Friday Sermon 21 June 1991
description:
The sermon being delivered now is a continuation of several previous ones that I have delivered before on the subject of Quranic prayers, prayers mentioned in these verses demonstrate how on certain occasions a statement of the conditions and circumstances becomes a prayer by themselves.  The Prophet Job (peace be upon him) only stated to Allah that he was stricken with pain and suffering.  Allah's response came immediately revealing to him a place for migration where he would be provided with cool and comforting water:

Remember Our servant Job, when he cried unto his Lord ‘Satan has inflicted me with toil and torment.’

'Spur on your mount.  Here is cool water to wash and to drink.'

(‘The Holy Quran’.  Sād: 42, 43).

While riding a means of transportation we must remember the prayer: Holy is Allah who made it our subject.*  Today's fast moving means are made useful to us only because God has empowered the humans to use them.  If we keep this fact in our hearts while travelling and make ourselves humble before the will of God, He will save us from harm and accidents.

Turning specifically to the American society, it is full of physical pollution which is very apparent.  But this society is choked with another most dangerous pollution which is working directly against the forces of the spiritual realm.  We can save ourselves from its evil effects only by saying this very prayer that I, have mentioned earlier. 

The Holy Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) had mentioned about the vicious character of the Antichrist, devoid of religion but deep and full of worldly matters.  We must deeply reflect upon the evil consequences of living in this polluted society and continuously pray to Allah to save us and our children from its evil consequences.

Towards the end I would mention the prayers of the Perfect Man. That Perfect Man is the Holy Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him):

We have enjoined on man to be good to his parents.  His mother bears him with pain and  brings him forth with pain.  His bearing and his weaning extends over thirty months, till when he attains his full maturity at forty years, he says ‘My Lord!  Grant me that I may be grateful for Thy favour which Thou hast bestowed upon me and upon my parents and that I may do such good deeds as may please Thee and make my offspring righteous too.  I do turn to Thee  and truly I am of the devout.’ 

(Al Ahqaf [The Dunes]: 16).

In this Prayer the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) thanks Allah for the favours granted him and his parents and asks the opportunity to do good deeds for himself and his progeny which means not merely his physical children but the entire Muslim Nation. 

The secret of acceptance of our prayers by Allah does not lie in mere repetition of words.  Our prayers will be accepted only if they are made in a spirit of devotion and sincerity as shown by the earlier Prophets.  Let us pray to Allah the Almighty that all of us become inheritors of the blessings which the Holy Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) asked for in his prayers.

* So that you may sit firmly upon their backs then when you are firmly seated thereon remember the favour of your Lord and say ‘Holy is He Who has subjected this to us as we by ourselves, were unable to harness it.’

'And to our Lord surely do we return.’

(Al–Zukhruf [The Gold Ornaments]: 14, 15).
